differential_diagnoses: Pyroglutamic acidosis (5-oxoprolinuria) due to chronic acetaminophen use and malnutrition
Sepsis (urosepsis from obstructive uropathy or bladder cancer)
Type B lactic acidosis associated with malignancy
Diabetic ketoacidosis or euglycemic ketoacidosis (secondary to dapagliflozin use)
Acute-on-chronic heart failure leading to hypoperfusion and Type A lactic acidosis
Toxic encephalopathy (due to recent medication changes or polypharmacy)
next_steps: Order serum lactate, ketones, and anion gap calculation
Perform renal ultrasound to assess for hydronephrosis and upper tract obstruction
Calculate corrected anion gap and delta-delta ratio
Obtain arterial blood gas to confirm acid-base status
Review medication administration record for potential drug-drug interactions or toxicity (e.g., dapagliflozin, morphine, diuretics)
Perform head CT to rule out intracranial pathology in the context of altered mental status

differential_diagnoses: Pyroglutamic acidosis (5-oxoprolinuria) due to chronic acetaminophen and N-acetylcysteine deficiency
Urosepsis secondary to bladder outlet obstruction
Type A lactic acidosis due to distributive/obstructive shock (RV failure)
Euglycemic or hyperglycemic ketoacidosis (SGLT2 inhibitor associated)
Adrenal insufficiency (secondary to chronic corticosteroid use)
next_steps: Calculate anion gap, delta-delta ratio, and serum osmolal gap
Obtain arterial blood gas (ABG) and serum beta-hydroxybutyrate levels
Perform CT pulmonary angiography (CTPA) to evaluate for pulmonary embolism
Perform renal/bladder ultrasound to exclude obstructive uropathy
Obtain random serum cortisol level and ACTH stimulation test if adrenal insufficiency is suspected
Review medication administration records for potential drug-induced causes of acidosis
